channel.title: The Triathlon Hour
channel.description: The Triathlon Hour is your podcast for everything triathlon. Pro triathlon news, training & racing. The worlds best triathlon coaches and experts. Love triathlon? Come on in.
channel.itunes:author: Jack Kelly

item.title: Road To Ironman 70.3 Oceanside - Episode 9
item.itunes:title: Road To Ironman 70.3 Oceanside - Episode 9
item.description: Shock news rocks the crew in the intro to start off this weeks episode of Road To Oceanside. After that the boys get stuck into their training weeks as always and then finish the show off by discussing the Norwegians and going deeper into the big announcement that was made to start the show.

item.title: Fred Funk Reveals The Biggest Mistake We Are ALL Making In Training
item.itunes:title: Fred Funk Reveals The Biggest Mistake We Are ALL Making In Training
item.description:
Fred gives us the secrets you need in training to ride well over Ironman 70.3 and Ironman distance as well as giving the specifics of how you should be doing your ride intervals to maximise them. He tells us the #1 mistake he thinks everyone makes in their training and we discuss volume vs intensity. We then get educated on how to train for running as a triathlete with run session specifics, the marathon vs half marathon training differences, brick sessions and treadmill training vs outdoor training. Then we discuss his favourite athlete, who the 20m draft rule will help and hurt, who is the athlete to watch in 2026 and lots of other general triathlon chat!

item.title: Road To Ironman 70.3 Oceanside - Episode 8
item.itunes:title: Road To Ironman 70.3 Oceanside - Episode 8
item.description: We are getting towards the pointy end of the journey to Oceanside 70.3 and this week Justin delivers some crazy power on the bike and big swim sessions, Jackson is doing bigger volume and more impressive training and Ari hits 30 hours for the second time this season and executes a near perfect week. The boys also go over the scheduling of their weeks and try to give each other advice on how they could improve it.
item.content:encoded: We are getting towards the pointy end of the journey to Oceanside 70.3 and this week Justin delivers some crazy power on the bike and big swim sessions, Jackson is doing bigger volume and more impressive training and Ari hits 30 hours for the second time this season and executes a near perfect week. The boys also go over the scheduling of their weeks and try to give each other advice on how they could improve it.

item.title: Jelle Geens - The Training Program To Win Ironman 70.3 World Championships
item.itunes:title: Jelle Geens - The Training Program To Win Ironman 70.3 World Championships
item.description:
After winning his second Ironman 70.3 World Championship Jelle Geens joins the podcast to give all the details about his training in the lead up to the race. After listening to this you can copy 99% of what he did in your own training or just get an appreciation for what he did. Jack also talks to Jelle about the rest of his 2025 season and plans for 2026.

item.title: Road To Ironman 70.3 Oceanside - Episode 7
item.itunes:title: Road To Ironman 70.3 Oceanside - Episode 7
item.description: Justin starts the show by talking about how disappointed he is in a certain group of people before the boys go on to talk about their best bike & run sessions from last week. There’s also some big news about Jackson firing one of his coaches  this week. And then the episode finishes with Ari’s coach joining the boys to be put under The Coaches Hotseat, a place where tough questions are asked and honest answers are expected.

item.title: Daniel Baekkegard Finally Speaks Out
item.itunes:title: Daniel Baekkegard Finally Speaks Out
item.description:
Daniel Baekkegard has finished on the podium of the Ironman 70.3 World Championships, the podium of T100 races and won multiple Ironman's. However it has felt like over the past 2 years he has gone missing slowly, bit by bit. Both in terms of racing results but also social media, Youtube, podcasts. Well as it turns out lots has been going on in Daniel's life, both professionally and personally and finally we get to hear all about it.

item.title: Marten Van Riel - “I Train 30-32 Hours Every Week.”
item.itunes:title: Marten Van Riel - “I Train 30-32 Hours Every Week.”
item.description:
It's been way, way too long but finally Marten Van Riel is back on the podcast. Epic training conversations about his high volume approach, love for long rides with long intervals in them and so much more. Combined with raw talk about his mental health battles and personal hardship in 2025. This is the kind of podcast that we started making them for when we did back in 2021. For you to get to know exactly how a world class athlete trains while also getting to know who they really are.

item.title: Road To Ironman 70.3 Oceanside - Episode 5
item.itunes:title: Road To Ironman 70.3 Oceanside - Episode 5
item.description: Justin, Jackson & Ari kick things off like every week by talking about their training for the week before Justin’s coach joins the pod to be put on the coaches hotseat! Ari & Jackson finally get a chance to ask Justin’s coach about why he does everything he does. If you aren’t following the boys journey’s to Oceanside 70.3, you’re missing out because it just keeps getting better & better every week.

item.title: Taylor Knibb - The Tell All Kona Collapse & T100 Contract Interview
item.itunes:title: Taylor Knibb - The Tell All Kona Collapse & T100 Contract Interview
item.description:
The things we love most about Taylor Knibb is her honesty, her detailed & analytical story telling and her contagious energy. Today’s podcast has all of that in spades. Taylor goes through her 2025 year in detail we’ve not come close to hearing anywhere else. From a tough start to the year with burn out, stress and T100 contract negotiations to the crazy events that unfolded when she collapsed with 2km to go whilst in the lead of the Ironman World Championships in Kona, we go into it all. Taylor was as honest and open as you’ll likely ever hear her and all we can do is thank her for that because it made for a podcast that’s a bit different from your standard one. We hope you enjoy it.

item.title: Road To Ironman 70.3 Oceanside - Episode 4
item.itunes:title: Road To Ironman 70.3 Oceanside - Episode 4
item.description: Justin, Jackson & Ari are back to bring you up to speed on the last week in their lives on the road to racing Ironman 70.3 Oceanside to open their 2026 triathlon seasons. Justin starts the show with an absolutely insane statement that could end the podcast, then they get into an hour of the best training and racing discussions so far on the series. Everything from big gear cycling to long runs to threshold farming. This season just keeps getting better and if you don’t love it after this episode, you’re a pretender.

item.title: Ranking The Best Ironman World Champions
item.itunes:title: Ranking The Best Ironman World Champions
item.description: Join Jack, Vicky, Appo & Greg as they discuss a recent article written by legendary coach Joel Filio about being too fit, too soon in the season. Then Jack ranks the best Ironman World Champions before the best part of every show - responding to your Spotify comments!

item.title: Road To Ironman 70.3 Oceanside - Episode 3
item.itunes:title: Road To Ironman 70.3 Oceanside - Episode 3
item.description: Jackson starts the show with a shocking & disturbing revelation before Ari & Justin gets involved to start talking about their training weeks. The three boys were all on different training camps this week - Justin undertook Tadej Pogacar levels of bike training, Ari continues his rise as the Zone 2 King and Jackson does even more VO2 Max. Plenty of insightful training talk and banter yet again this week.

item.title: Zone 2 Training is DUMB
item.itunes:title: Zone 2 Training is DUMB
item.description:
This week we starts things off by discussing zone 2 training, how to be a good time trialist, polarised training with high volume and ERG mode on the trainer. We then talk about food & diet for a little bit before a short but interesting discussion of what is the most important run session of the week. And, as always, we finish the show off by reading and responding to your Spotify comments!

item.title: Train Insane Over Christmas!
item.itunes:title: Train Insane Over Christmas!
item.description:
How to train over Christmas, what to do, what not to do, tips & secret bits of wisdom start off the pod before then diving into the world of coaching in triathlon. The crew all set each other 2026 training/racing goals that we must be kept accountable to, the final Pretendies categories are announced and we read your Spotify comments. There’s some podcast beef that gets addressed, the founder of an influential training app gets called a fraud, pornstars/only fans, what data metrics to use on race day and lots more. This pod goes everywhere really.

item.title: Lies & Deception Uncovered In the Triathlon World.
item.itunes:title: Lies & Deception Uncovered In the Triathlon World.
item.description:
We start this weeks episode by going over everything from the T100 Grand Final on the weekend. We then move onto the T100’s 2026 plans and their big announcement of a merger with World Triathlon in 2027. To say there are some strong opinions thrown around in these discussions would be an understatement. Then it’s time to talk about the uncovering that a triathlete has been deceiving and lying to everyone since at least 2024. And we finish off the show, as always, by reading and responding to your Spotify comments.

item.title: The Biggest Triathlon Overreactions
item.itunes:title: The Biggest Triathlon Overreactions
item.description:
After a short discussion on how to feel good post travel for training/races and a quick discussion on having sex during race week the crew dive into the main topic for today’s podcast - “real or overreaction” where someone makes a statement and everyone judges if it is a real or an overreaction and gives their reason why. It ends up being a pretty brutally honest and at times controversial segment that was more interesting than everyone even anticipated. After that the guys read your Spotify comments and finish with an exciting announcement.

From who is to blame and who is in the wrong to who were the big winners from the situation - we don’t hold back. We then have a brief chat about Ironman Arizona and finish the episode with an exciting announcement!

item.title: There Is A Serial Killer In Marbella While The 70.3 World Champs Are On
item.itunes:title: There Is A Serial Killer In Marbella While The 70.3 World Champs Are On
item.description:
It’s day 4 and the final day of the Ironman 70.3 World Championships daily pods and in today’s episode Jack tells the boys about a string of murders that have been taking place in Marbella where the race is happening including a dead body being dumped in the swim course earlier during race week. The boys then give their final thoughts and predictions for the race, give you their best training & general triathlon tips and read your Spotify comments!

item.description:
Day 3 of the Ironman 70.3 World Champs daily is here and today we have a special guest for the first 30 minutes while we read your Spotify comments and address some key race predictions. Appo’s then brings his personal sports scientist onto the podcast who answers all our questions on how to fuel/hydrate your training and racing. And to round out the show it’s f***, marry, kill time.

item.title: The Kona Debrief Podcast!
item.itunes:title: The Kona Debrief Podcast!
item.description:
Wow. The Ironman World Championships in Kona on the weekend were insane. The highest of highs, near death experiences, chaos, drama, theatre. Kona had it all. We decided that with this debrief podcast we wanted to leave no stone unturned and talk about everything. So we went a bit longer than usual to make sure we did that. We hope this satisfies your thirst for Kona content coming off what was one of the wildest races in the history of triathlon.

Day 3 of the Kona Daily pods is here and the boys + Vicky are joined by the defending Ironman World Champion Laura Philipp to discuss her race, her preparation and her rivals! item.title: Taylor Knibb Joins Us To Kick Off The Kona Daily Podcast Wars.
item.itunes:title: Taylor Knibb Joins Us To Kick Off The Kona Daily Podcast Wars.
item.description:
Day 1 of the daily podcasts leading into the Ironman World Championships is here and we are lucky enough to be joined by Taylor Knibb to discuss the race. The boys give us their top 5 people with most to lose at Kona this year list, they discuss who the big names are that can actually win the race as well as having some strong debate on why some women can’t win the race that might surprise you. It’s going to be a massive week of daily pods and we are just getting started!

item.itunes:title: Hayden Wilde Is A Pretender + Is Science in Training Overrated?!
item.description:
The boys are back and we’ve got Hayden Wilde drama and T100 Spain to talk about off the top of the show. Lucy Charles Barclay is on the boys radar and then we dive into what actually happened between Olav Aleksander Bu and the Norwegians. Speaking of the Norwegians, we have some questions that need addressing about them including science in training and Kristian’s approach as head coach. Then the boys wrap up the show with some pledges for you all!

item.title: The Mastermind Behind The Norwegian Method - Arild Tveiten
item.itunes:title: The Mastermind Behind The Norwegian Method - Arild Tveiten
item.description:
Following the Ironman World Champs podium clean sweep by The Norwegian’s (Casper Stornes, Gustav Iden & Kristian Blummenfelt) we talk to Arild Tveiten, the man who started it all, built it all and without whom non of it would have happened. However there have been several tough times in the process, including Kristian & Gustav choosing to leave Arild’s program, money getting in the way of what they’d built together, Olav Aleksander Bu’s role in the program, Casper and Kristian/Gustav not training together for a couple of years before ultimately coming back together in 2025 and the group once again finding their way to the top of the world. This podcast is the first in a long time that we’ve done which isn’t just an interview; rather, it tells a story. And that story is a long, interesting, successful and at times difficult one.

item.title: The Norwegians DOMINATE The Ironman World Championships.
item.itunes:title: The Norwegians DOMINATE The Ironman World Championships.
item.description:
What a week it’s been. What a race the Ironman World Champs ended up being, surpassing all our wildest expectations for how crazy it would play out. The Norwegians dominated and we break that down as well as ALL the action, stories and talking points from the day. We try to bring you some laughs and strong opinions along the way as well. Again, a huge shout out to all of you for making this the best week in the podcast’s short history. We can’t express enough how grateful we are.

item.title: T100 CEO Sam Renouf Addresses “Tough” 2025
item.itunes:title: T100 CEO Sam Renouf Addresses “Tough” 2025
item.description:
CEO of the T100 Sam Renouf joins us to talk about everything that’s been happening in the T100 sphere.  He addresses the France course change, Valencia’s last minute cancellation and location change, the Vegas cancellation and change to Wollongong, their financial struggles & if they were the reason athletes 2024 end of year bonuses weren’t paid until now, whether having races in the Middle East is purely for money, whether the T100 model is better than the PTO Open model of 2023, will athletes contracts be released and whether the contract model is working, field sizes, will the T100 seriously be going to the Olympics and will they be taking over Alp D’Huez Triathlon, who does Sam Renouf answer to for his performance and how do the T100 come to the decisions they come to. There’s also lots more in there to look forward to and hopefully we asked every question you could possibly want!

item.title: Monday’s Triathlon Hour - Full Show - August 18th
item.itunes:title: Monday’s Triathlon Hour - Full Show - August 18th
item.description:
The Triathlon Hour Hotseat opens up today’s podcast where the only rule is you have to give your honest answer to the questions asked. Some of those questions are about Collin Chartier & Tomas Rodriguez’s potential imminent return to racing, who has the most annoying Instagram of any pro triathlete, is triathlon too soft, has there been a softening of our stance on Ironman, Jack’s real thoughts on Scott Derue & Andrew Messick, who is Appo attracted to, why has Jack really not been on the T100 broadcast, what’s the worst thing about living in the Boulder triathlon bubble, why did Lucy Charles Barclay block Jack on Instagram, is commenting on triathlete’s body weight fair game and lots more. We then do another edition of “who is under pressure” in the triathlon world, have some training discussion and pick which 3 triathletes we would all pick to race for our lives over the Ironman distance.

item.description:
Joe Skipper sits down for his first ever episode hosting his own podcast on The Triathlon Hour and for the occasion he’s joined by the superstar Taylor Knibb. Joe & Taylor have a conversation that makes you feel like you’re sitting back on a couch around the fire with them after a day on training camp and just listening to them both share stories and have a laugh. Whether you’re out training while you listening, driving your car, on the couch having a warm beverage or doing some housework you’ll definitely love this one.

Boise 70.3 & Ironman Leeds get a quick discussion and then things you NEED to add into your training or do more of and contender or pretender is back!

item.title: Kristian Hogenhaug - ALL His Cycling & Running Training Secrets Revealed!
item.itunes:title: Kristian Hogenhaug - ALL His Cycling & Running Training Secrets Revealed!
item.description:
We start with the Ironman Frankfurt penalty drama from Kristian's perspective, his power numbers from the race to get a 9 minute lead off the bike and his intentional weight loss for the race. Then we go into ALL of his 2025 bike training Monday to Friday including session details + power numbers. ERG mode vs no ERG mode on the trainer, indoor training vs outdoor training, his Kona power numbers last year and some other race power numbers, his running training including all of his session/volume/long run details. His sleep, his problems with low sex drive + erectile dysfunction in the past and some really honest chat about getting draft assistance by lead motorbikes/cars on course at Ironman races, including this weekend at Ironman Frankfurt. This is the best pure training pod of 2025 so far.

item.title: The Ironman Frankfurt Drama & Chaos!!!
item.itunes:title: The Ironman Frankfurt Drama & Chaos!!!
item.description:
Ironman Frankfurt happened over the weekend and it was full of chaos, drama and world class performances. There were disqualifications given, then taken back. Penalties galore. Arguments between athletes/athlete teams and referees on the course. Race changing mechanicals. Cramping/shoe issues. The Norwegian hype train was back in full force and dominating on the world stage, insane bike performances and so much more. This is one of the most action packed podcast of the year so far.

 

We put a lot of time & energy into bringing you this podcast every week and have done for 4 years now. If you appreciate what we do, we would love your support and you can do so for less than the cost of a coffee per week on Patreon  - Patreon link

item.title: Sam Long Reveals Every Detail of His Secret Training Camp!!!
item.itunes:title: Sam Long Reveals Every Detail of His Secret Training Camp!!!
item.description:
Last week Sam Long undertook an intensive 5 day swim training camp ran by the best swimmer in triathlon Greg Harper. On today’s podcast both Sam & Greg join us to tell us every single detail of what happened on that camp. All I asked of them before we did the podcast was “can you try and make all of us listening feel like we were there at the camp?” and I think that’s exactly what Sam & Greg achieved in this podcast. This is the kind of podcast we’ve never really had a lot of in triathlon before and is what I’m most passionate about bringing you all. I really hope you enjoy it!

item.title: We NEED To Talk About Lionel Sanders
item.itunes:title: We NEED To Talk About Lionel Sanders
item.description:
We start the show like always with our triathlon news headlines of the week. Then we dive into Lionel Sanders and the massive conversation he opened up last week. We share some embarrassing (but completely relatable) stories of our own. Then it’s time to close the show with the first ever funeral hosted live on a triathlon podcast. History is made on today’s episode.

 

00:00 - Triathlon News Headlines. 

 

26:03 - Lionel Sanders, opening up & embarrassing/vulnerable stories. 

 

1:05:20 - We host a funeral live on the podcast.

item.title: A Big Weekend of Drama & Controversy at Ironman 70.3 Eagleman
item.itunes:title: A Big Weekend of Drama & Controversy at Ironman 70.3 Eagleman
item.description:
Jack, Justin & Ari are back to bring you your weekly source of triathlon entertainment. The racing & drama from Ironman 70.3 Eagleman is on the agenda including the controversy surrounding the pro men interfering with the pro women’s race and Sam Long v Trevor Foley. Sam Appleton joins us on the pod filling in for Greg and he questions whether the T100 has lived up to expectations or not. He also brings his “10 Commandments of Triathlon” based on his two decades in triathlon and the group delivers a therapy session for Ari. We then finish with some inspirational quotes and rapping Lose Yourself by Eminem. 

Sign up to Patreon Here - Patreon link

item.title: Dr. Dan Plews - Ironman World Champion Coach Talks All Things Training & Nutrition
item.itunes:title: Dr. Dan Plews - Ironman World Champion Coach Talks All Things Training & Nutrition
item.description:
Dan Plews is an expert in training & nutrition with 20 years of experience in the physiology & sports science space.  He was an Ironman World Championship Age Grpup athlete himself and coached Chelsea Sodaro to winning the professional Ironman World Championship. This is 90 minutes of pure training & nutrition conversation for us die hards.

item.title: Justin Riele!
item.itunes:title: Justin Riele!
item.description:
Justin Riele joins us to talk all things training, triathlon and life. How did he become such a beast on the bike while still working full time? He gives all the details on his unique and specific training methods. He talks about race visualisation, lactate and the Norwegian method, structuring training around his job and being a weekend warrior. He also gives us exact numbers on how much money he makes from triathlon with races and brand sponsorship which is very unique and honest insight. The highlight of the pod is definitely Justin singing a few bars of Mariah Carey.

item.title: ALEX YEE!!!
item.itunes:title: ALEX YEE!!!
item.description:
Alex Yee joins us to talk about his training & life in details you’ve never heard before. He goes through day by day what his typical training week looked like prior to running London Marathon, including key sessions and why he did things the way he did and compared that to his typical week leading into winning Olympic gold at Paris in the triathlon. We talk about his future plans for potentially trying to break the Ironman full distance world record in a sub 6:44 challenge and going to Kona. Eliot Kipchoge, the half marathon world record, screen time, relationships, the universe and so much more training conversation make up what was a very, very special podcast for The Triathlon Hour.

item.title: Joe Skipper Returns To The Podcast After 2 Years!!!!
item.itunes:title: Joe Skipper Returns To The Podcast After 2 Years!!!!
item.description:
Joe Skipper joins us for the first time in almost 2 years. We catch up on what's been happening in his training, racing and life over that time. Other things on the agenda are the massive training weekend he just did, can you be Ironman World Champion if you do the T100 World Tour, lots of detailed run training chat, can Joe get back to his best, some walking down memory lane about Joe's best races and what training led to them, Triathlon Mockery, three deep questions that get Joe being serious and lots, lots more!

item.title: Kate Waugh!!!!!
item.itunes:title: Kate Waugh!!!!!
item.description:
Kate Waugh won Singapore T100 on the weekend by almost 7 minutes - the biggest winning margin in T100 history so far AND she did it on debut against the likes of Ash Gentle, Julie Derron and Lucy Charles-Barclay. We talk about the race, how it played out from Kate’s perspective, her move to long course, what it’s like behind the scenes at the T100 and then we do a BIG TIME deep dive into Kate’s training she did this year from her early VO2 max/polarised block into her 6 week LT2/specific block leading into the race. 

 

Sign up to Patreon Here - Patreon link

item.content:encoded:
Kate Waugh won Singapore T100 on the weekend by almost 7 minutes - the biggest winning margin in T100 history so far AND she did it on debut against the likes of Ash Gentle, Julie Derron and Lucy Charles-Barclay. We talk about the race, how it played out from Kate’s perspective, her move to long course, what it’s like behind the scenes at the T100 and then we do a BIG TIME deep dive into Kate’s training she did this year from her early VO2 max/polarised block into her 6 week LT2/specific block leading into the race. 

 

Sign up to Patreon Here - Patreon link

item.title: Olav Aleksander Bu - Genius Coach of Kristian Blummenfelt & Gustav Iden
item.itunes:title: Olav Aleksander Bu - Genius Coach of Kristian Blummenfelt & Gustav Iden
item.description:
One of the great sports science & triathlon coaching minds is back on The Triathlon Hour. We talk about a lot of training topics including intensity control and how crucial it is to being your best, how important is volume in training and can doing less with more intensity/recovery be better for your training or are you better off to focus on trying to do more, peak human performance, what is Zone 2 training and how do the Norwegian's use it in their training and then we hit some quick fire questions about long running/riding, should you use super shoes in your training and should VO2 max training be a part of everyone's programs. If you love training - this podcast with Olav is for you.

item.title: Els Visser - Old School Training Secrets!!!
item.itunes:title: Els Visser - Old School Training Secrets!!!
item.description:
In this episode we go straight into how Els trains. It's very unique. She does lots of double bike days and double/triple run days which we discuss as well as talking about her unique approach to long rides. We talk big gear riding, build rides, hill reps on the run, speed/track running, using a treadmill in your running and how Els approaches her long runs. Els gives us an entire breakdown of her weekly training structure and how she adjusts it day to day based on her coaches text messages. We discuss training based on feeling, the importance of wanting to work hard & suffer in training and deciding not to train using "modern" scientific approaches like lactate, HR, power, etc. We also have a quick chat about Els love languages.

item.title: Greg Harper - The Best Swimmer in Long Course Triathlon???
item.itunes:title: Greg Harper - The Best Swimmer in Long Course Triathlon???
item.description:
Greg Harper is arguably the best swimmer in long course triathlon and has taken it upon himself to become swim advisor to the bad pro swimmers. He’s also on the journey himself of trying to ride & run at the level required to capitalise on his swim so that he can start winning big races. Today we talk about how he would fix Lionel Sanders & Sam Long’s swims, hear all about his own training (ride & run, mainly) and chat about our love lives.

If you want to hear all about her run training, run sessions, long runs, weekly run structure, bike power, bike sessions, bike training structure and many, many more training details then THIS is the podcast for you. item.title: Hayden Wilde is BACK and sharing all his secrets!!!
item.itunes:title: Hayden Wilde is BACK and sharing all his secrets!!!
item.description:
The King joins us for the first time in over a year and we had SO much to talk about. Some big coaching changes which Hayden talks about in detail and gives a crazy amount of info on his new training program, sessions, structure, etc. We talk Ironman 70.3 World Champs, T100, Paris Olympics silver medal. All of it. Hayden also weighs in on which side he's picking in some long course beef. When I sat down and decided what I wanted my podcasts to be like in 2025, this episode is basically EXACTLY what I wanted so I can't wait for you to all listen.

item.title: Fred Funk - Zone 2, VO2 Max, Ride & Run Volume!
item.itunes:title: Fred Funk - Zone 2, VO2 Max, Ride & Run Volume!
item.description:
Fred joins us this week to talk allllll about training. We talk about his swim training in Australia with the famous swim coach JR, how the sessions look, their structure & his favourite session he's done there so far. We then spend a lot of time talking about his bike training. VO2 max, VLaMax, Z2 training, long rides, periodisation, fuelling. All of it. And then we talk about his running, run volume, run intensity, VO2 max sessions in running and the long run. This one is for all of you training nerds.

item.title: Morgan Pearson!!!
item.itunes:title: Morgan Pearson!!!
item.description:
The King of Zone 2 (who cares) joins us for the first time ever on The Triathlon Hour! We start the episode by getting to know Morgan a little bit, how he got offered a T100 contract, who by, when it happened, what his plans for 2025 with running, short course and T100 are, his plans for Ironman and his plans for the LA Olympics. We then go into his training and talk about how he trains, his coach Ryan Bolton and what will be needed in his training to have success over middle distance. Things then take a bit of a turn and we talk about the famous Zone 2 meme, the "who cares" meme and Morgan talks about his 2020 rivalry with Sam Long and his potential 2025 rivalry with Jelle Geens. As with everything Morgan does, this was a fun one!!!

item.itunes:title: Chelsea Sodaro
item.description:
2022 Ironman World Champion Chelsea Sodaro joins us to talk about her training with new coach, Neal Henderson, and how it has changed in 2024/the start of 2025 compared to her old coach, Dan Plews. We talk about the highs & lows of 2024, 2025 race plans, dating apps, American celebrities/ podcasts and why Chelsea REFUSES to talk about something big.

item.title: Taylor Knibb - Ironman 70.3 World Champion!!!!
item.itunes:title: Taylor Knibb - Ironman 70.3 World Champion!!!!
item.description:
Episode 2 of 2025 is here and we are joined by the middle distance GOAT and newly crowned Ironman 70.3 World Champion/T100 World Champion Taylor Knibb. We go deep on her training, particularly the new training approach she had in 2024 with coach Dan Lorang. We talk about weekly structure, bike workouts, long rides, long runs, run week structure, sessions, etc etc etc. You name it, we talked about it. We also have a bit of a laugh about the "new" Taylor Knibb who isn't afraid to speak her mind. And if anyone wants to know what power numbers Taylor Knibb rides, then listen closely to this episode as we try to get to the bottom of it.

item.title: Jelle Geens - Ironman 70.3 World Champion!!!
item.itunes:title: Jelle Geens - Ironman 70.3 World Champion!!!
item.description:
We are joined by the newly crowned Ironman 70.3 World Champion Jelle Geens for our first podcast of 2025. We talk all about the training that led Jelle to be able to win the race, going particularly deep on the run training that took him to a 1:07 half marathon off the bike. We also get into the nitty gritty on his bike training and how he and his coach train. There is a detailed race recap from the 70.3 worlds from Jelle’s perspective and some conversation about his plans for 2025 balancing T100 and Ironman racing.

item.title: Kyle Smith - Ironman 70.3 World Championships
item.itunes:title: Kyle Smith - Ironman 70.3 World Championships
item.description:
Kyle Smith joins us to talk about the Ironman 70.3 World Championships that are happening this weekend. We talk about his training in the lead up including lots of specific sessions & training structures. We talk about race tactics and his race day plans. Body weight and ideal race weight are on the table. Who are the biggest threats to Kyle’s chances of winning his first 70.3 Worlds and lots, lots more!

item.title: Marten Van Riel - Ironman Cozumel Drama & Becoming T100 World Champion
item.itunes:title: Marten Van Riel - Ironman Cozumel Drama & Becoming T100 World Champion
item.description:
Marten Van Riel joins us today to talk about the craziness of his debut Ironman in Cozumel where he was leading at 165km after having been solo all day before crashing his bike and colliding with a young girl on the side of the bike course. He went on to have the fastest marathon of the day running 2:39 but because of the time spent on the side of the rode he didn’t win or get his Ironman World Championship qualifying slot. We hear all the details from Marten in his first podcast since it happened. We also do a season debrief where we talk about his incredible season on the T100 World Tour becoming the first ever world champion and showing everyone that he is a force over middle distance.

The Feed - Your one stop shop for all your triathlon needs http://www.thefeed.com/triathlonhour

item.itunes:title: Dan Lorang - Coach of Taylor Knibb, Lucy Charles-Barclay, Anne Haug, Kyle Smith & Fred Funk
item.description:
The greatest triathlon coach of the modern era joins us to talk about all things triathlon! We talk about his last few months coaching Jan Frodeno, go through all of his current athletes and the training they are doing, we talk women's Ironman World Champs, which male and females would perform well if they swapped to World Tour cycling and much, much more!

item.title: Chris Lieto
item.itunes:title: Chris Lieto
item.description:
Today we are joined by triathlon legend, Chris Lieto. Chris is best known for his iconic races at the Ironman World Championships, particularly coming 2nd place on a day where he road off the front of the race. Chris is also a triathlon coach now and has recently begun working with some pro long course triathletes.

item.title: Tomas Rodriguez Hernandez Tests Positive for Doping!
item.itunes:title: Tomas Rodriguez Hernandez Tests Positive for Doping!
item.description:
In today’s episode we do a deep dive on the positive doping test returned by Tomas Rodriguez. What is clomophine? Why do people take it? When was the test? We dissect his performances, pulling out of races in the past couple of months, how Tomas maintains his innocence and is it possible for chicken and eggs to be the reason he tests positive? TUE’s and suspect coaches also come into the conversation. This is the deep dive you’ve been wanting since you heard about Tomas’s positive drug test!

The Feed - Your one stop shop for all your triathlon needs http://www.thefeed.com/triathlonhour

item.title: Lionel Sanders Will NOT Win The Ironman World Champs? (Ironman Lake Placid Recap)
item.itunes:title: Lionel Sanders Will NOT Win The Ironman World Champs? (Ironman Lake Placid Recap)
item.description:
In today’s podcast we give you the ultimate Ironman Lake Placid recap. We go deep on Lionel Sanders race + what it means for the future. We do the same for Joe Skipper, Braden Currie & the “old guard” of Ironman racing. We talk about the carnage of the women’s race. And we finish by addressing Ironman’s coverage. **Warning: we thought it was bad.**

item.title: Cam Wurf
item.itunes:title: Cam Wurf
item.description:
Cam Wurf joins us to talk about the Sam Laidlow drama, his race at Ironman Vitoria-Gasteiz on the weekend, his plans & training leading into this years Ironman World Championships, the hunt he's been on for Max Neumann, what he's heard about Kristian Blummenfelt's move to professional cycling and his take on Lionel Sanders being coached by Jan Frodeno.

item.title: The Chase Pack - Episode 12
item.itunes:title: The Chase Pack - Episode 12
item.description:
It’s an all lads episode again this week!  The boys discuss the impact body weight has on males for performance in triathlon. Harry’s massive two week altitude camp comes to an end. How often should you wear super shoes? Does Andy wear his Alphafly’s to the shops? Why do UK drivers hate cyclists so much? And the boys go through their weeks and upcoming races!

The Feed - Your one stop shop for all your triathlon needs https://thefeed.com/triathlonhour

item.title: The Ultimate Ironman 70.3 Oceanside Preview Show
item.itunes:title: The Ultimate Ironman 70.3 Oceanside Preview Show
item.description:
Absolutely nothing is left out in this chat about Ironman 70.3 Oceanside. It might be the longest, most detailed pre race breakdown/conversation that has ever been recorded in the triathlon world. 80% of it is serious, 10% is silly and 10% is somewhere in the middle. If you like podcasts that feel like you’re listening to 2 friends talk about your favourite topic (triathlon) for over 2 hours, this episode is for you!

item.itunes:title: The Chase Pack - Episode 8
item.description:
On this week's episode of The Chase Pack Penny goes into detail about het Ironman 70.3 Geelong race including all the details about her penalty. We find out how Aris swimming has progressed since his Australian Method camp, Ari also begins the episode with a rather controversial conspiracy about the shampoo industry! Find out about Harrys hangover and how Annas Oceanside prep is progressing. All this and more on The Chase Pack!

item.content:encoded:
On this week's episode of The Chase Pack Penny goes into detail about het Ironman 70.3 Geelong race including all the details about her penalty. item.itunes:title: The Breadwinners - Episode 1
item.description:
The Breadwinners is a brand new podcast series hosted by professional triathletes Emma Pallant-Browne, Imogen Simmonds &  Amelia Watkinson. All 3 women are ranked inside the top 20 in the world and will be racing in the inaugural PTO T100 Tour. In this episode the girls discuss the upcoming T100 series, their training schedules, drug testing and much more! Tune in every fortnight to follow along their journeys in 2024.

item.itunes:title: The Chase Pack - Episode 4
item.description:
On this week's episode of The Chase Pack Penny gives an in-depth breakdown of her 4th place performance at IRONMAN 70.3 Tasmania including having a stack on the bike! The girls have left, and Ari is now solo on his Australian Method Training camp in Ballarat, we find out how his swim is progressing with his new swim heavy schedule. Is the indoor pool longer than 50 metres or is Ari losing his mind...?  Harry gives us the ins and outs of his build to Ironman South Africa. All this and more on The Chase Pack!!

item.itunes:title: David Tilbury-Davis - Coach of Ash Gentle & Ex-Coach of Lionel Sanders
item.description:
David Tilbury-Davis is one of long course triathlons most successful coaches. He is the current coach of Ash Gentle, Josh Amberger, Skye Moench, Amelia Watkinson and was the former coach of Lionel Sanders during 2017, arguably his greatest ever year, culminating in his famous 2nd place finish at the Ironman World Championships to Patrick Lange.

item.description:
Olav Aleksander Bu is the world famous triathlon coach of Kristian Blummenfelt & Gustav Iden. In todays podcast we do a breakdown on both Kristian & Gustav’s 2023 years and then talk about what both of them will be doing in 2024. This is a DEEP training conversation, so if that’s your thing, this podcast is for you.

item.title: Marten Van Riel - The Man Who Will Take Over From The Norwegians?
item.itunes:title: Marten Van Riel - The Man Who Will Take Over From The Norwegians?
item.description:
Marten Van Riel is the man everyone thinks could start dominating long course triathlon. He talks all about his shift to long course racing from short course, racing the PTO series & Ironman 70.3 Worlds in 2024, who he sees as his competition and lots more. We go over whether the Norwegians will still be the people to beat, whether the Olympics matters more than the big long course triathlon races and Fred Funk’s Only Fans is brought up as always. We are also joined by friend of the show Aaron Royle for the chat. 

Sign up to PATREON to listen to bonus weekly exclusive podcasts + support The Triathlon Hour here -  https://www.patreon.com/thetriathlonhour

item.title: Skye Moench - The Story Of Being Told To Lose 3-4kg By Her Coach
item.itunes:title: Skye Moench - The Story Of Being Told To Lose 3-4kg By Her Coach
item.description:
Skye Moench comes on and breaks her silence about why she left her coach of 5 years following a string of comments about her weight, specifically her need to lose weight in order to be the best in the world. Skye takes us through the whole story but small warning it gets quite deep & personal on a sensitive issue. 

Sign up to PATREON to listen every week to bonus podcasts (and support The Triathlon Hour) here -  https://www.patreon.com/thetriathlonhour

item.title: The Reality of Triathlon That We NEVER Talk About ... But Need To Talk About.
item.itunes:title: The Reality of Triathlon That We NEVER Talk About ... But Need To Talk About.
item.description:
Following big races like the Ironman 70.3 World Champs we always focus on the people who had amazing days. But for this episode we talk to Steve McKenna who didn’t have a good day. In fact, he DNF’d. And we are joined by his partner to discuss the obvious warning signs leading into a race that he wasn’t going to have a good race.

item.itunes:title: PTO US Open: Behind The Scenes Untold Stories + US Open Race Recap and Asian Open Preview.
item.description:
We start today’s episode by taking you behind the scenes at the PTO US Open with some stories you would never usually hear that happened throughout the week and during the race weekend. Some are funny, some are serious and some are in between. We then bring in Craig Alexander & Mirinda Carfrae to do a recap of the racing at the PTO US Open and preview the PTO Asian Open that takes place in a weeks time.

item.title: Kristian Blummenfelt- Road to the PTO US Open
item.itunes:title: Kristian Blummenfelt- Road to the PTO US Open
item.description:
Kristian is the #1 ranked PTO triathlete in the world and he joins us today to talk all things the PTO US Open. We talk about what he has to do to win after coming 2nd in both of his PTO Open appearances, the race dynamics, who he’s worried about, he dissects all the big favourites and their roles in the race. We also have some training chat, future racing chat (including Kristian revealing some big news about 2024) & life updates.

item.title: Andrew Messick - Retired or Asked To Step Down? The Hard Questions on Hamburg, Ironman World Champs, Race Entry Cost & Lots More.
item.itunes:title: Andrew Messick - Retired or Asked To Step Down? The Hard Questions on Hamburg, Ironman World Champs, Race Entry Cost & Lots More.
item.description:
I sat down with Andrew Messick in an exclusive, one on one podcast following his announcement that he is stepping down from his role as the CEO of Ironman. I ask everything we want answered like did Andrew retire or get asked to step down? The hard questions about Hamburg. Race entry cost & race entry refunds. The Nice world champs low entry numbers. Who will be the new CEO? And  the big one... what was Andrew eating on our last podcast together?

*Sign up to PATREON to support the show (for only $1 USD per week it is the main reason the show is still going. We bloody appreciate it!) -https://www.patreon.com/howtheytrain/

item.title: The Coaches - Bjorn Geesmann & David Tilbury-Davis
item.itunes:title: The Coaches - Bjorn Geesmann & David Tilbury-Davis
item.description:
Episode 2 of The Coaches features two of the best in the business, Bjorn Geesmann and David Tilbury-Davis. Bjorn coaches Patrick Lange, Kat Matthews and a handful of other athletes whilst David is the coach of Ashleigh Gentle, Skye Moench and the longest serving coach of Lionel Sanders. 

Be a fly on the wall for this insightful conversation about the impacts of AI on coaching, the logistics of managing and training athletes, money in Triathlon, doping in sport and much more!  

*Sign up to PATREON to support the show (for only $1 USD per week it is the main reason the show is still going. We bloody appreciate it!) -https://www.patreon.com/howtheytrain/

Richard Murray joins the boys for this week's episode of the Hayden Wilde Triathlon Show. 

Richard goes over his career and what shaped him into the athlete he is today including his 4th place finish at the Rio Olympics. The boys talk about Zwift weight dopers, the recent French Grand Prix and all things short course triathlon!

item.title: Hayden Wilde’s Triathlon Show with Jonny Brownlee
item.itunes:title: Hayden Wilde’s Triathlon Show with Jonny Brownlee
item.description:
Jonny Brownlee joins the boys for the best episode of Hayden Wilde’s Triathlon Show yet. They talk about the iconic Cozumel race where Jonny was carried the last 500m of the race by his brother Alistair, the 2012 London Olympics where Jonny came 3rd and Jonny tells two stories for the first time ever - one about the Brownlee brothers allegedly paying off a teammate to sabotage their biggest rival of all times race and one about dropping his brother Alistair in a track session right before the Rio Olympics and knowing he was in better shape than him. 

PILLAR PERFORMANCE - https://pillarperformance.shop/pages/htt-podcast EVERYTHING HAS 20% AUTOMATICALLY DISCOUNTED OFF IT AT CHECKOUT.

item.description:
The biggest name in female long course triathlon, and close to the biggest star in triathlon full stop, Lucy Charles-Barclay joins us today. item.title: Road to the PTO European Open - The Final Episode | Race Week & Race Recap.
item.itunes:title: Road to the PTO European Open - The Final Episode | Race Week & Race Recap.
item.description:
The last episode in what has been one of the craziest triathlon stories you’ll ever see is here. The boys all raced the PTO European Open on the weekend and come on to talk about their weeks in the lead up and then go into all the details about their races and the race itself. What a whirlwind series this has been. I truly don’t think anyone saw this series becoming what it did. On behalf of Fred, Aaron, Tom & Mika we thank you for listening every week and following the journey.

Precision Fuel & Hydration link https://visit.pfandh.com/howtheytrain-code

I recommend PF 90, PF 30 and the 1500 electrolyte tabs.

item.title: Road to the PTO European Open - Collin Chartier BEHIND THE SCENES + Race Week Chat
item.itunes:title: Road to the PTO European Open - Collin Chartier BEHIND THE SCENES + Race Week Chat
item.description:
Fred, Aaron, Mika, Tom & Myself go into all the behind the scenes details on Collin Chartier. Probably give a bit too much of an inside look into the whole situation. Then we move onto training weeks, then the PTO European Open this weekend and how the boys think it will play out and go deep on the race. This episode is massive. 2 hours we needed for it all and never a dull moment.

Precision Fuel & Hydration - Use the code HTT23 for 15% off at checkout https://visit.pfandh.com/howtheytrain-code

I recommend PF 90, PF 30 and the 1500 electrolyte tabs.

item.title: The Triathlon Hour with SAM LAIDLOW
item.itunes:title: The Triathlon Hour with SAM LAIDLOW
item.description:
Welcome to the first ever episode of The Triathlon Hour. In this weeks episode with Sam we discuss Doping in triathlon, his withdrawal from the PTO European Open + his predictions on how it will go down as well as his plans to win the Ironman World Champs this year. This is a How They Train podcast dedicated specifically to triathlon news, current affairs, race previews, recaps or analysis and all things triathlon that are not training related. How They Train will still be happening once a week, we are just having a break from it this week to launch this new series. We also plan to have a third special series run alongside How They Train and The Triathlon Hour so you will always be getting 2-3 episodes per week.

item.title: Collin Chartier - The Exclusive “Tell All” E.P.O Doping Interview
item.itunes:title: Collin Chartier - The Exclusive “Tell All” E.P.O Doping Interview
item.description: Collin Chartier tested positive for EPO in an out of competition drug test in March 2023. This information became public knowledge today. He has been banned from competing in professional sport for 3 years. This is the interview where he tells us everything.

item.title: Road to the PTO European Open - Episode 9
item.itunes:title: Road to the PTO European Open - Episode 9
item.description:
On this week’s episode of Road to the PTO European Open Tom has a verbal altercation with the weather, Mika is putting in the work aqua jogging but believes some of the grandmas might be cheating by using their arms to drop him, Aaron goes over his preparation for Challenge Mogan-Gran Canaria this weekend and Fred talks about the effects of altitude on his training. The boys reveal a special scoop for the podcast about a certain device that will be used in Ibiza! They go over their top 3 female podium predictions, both start lists, Jan being back, who’s missing and much more!  

 

*Sign up to PATREON to support the show (for only $2 AUD/$1 USD per week it is the main reason the show is still going. We bloody appreciate it!) -https://www.patreon.com/howtheytrain/

item.title: Flora Duffy - Ironman, Olympic Gold & Winning 12 x Triathlon World Championships
item.itunes:title: Flora Duffy - Ironman, Olympic Gold & Winning 12 x Triathlon World Championships
item.description:
Is Flora Duffy the greatest female triathlete of all time? She’s definitely in the discussion and she joins us tomorrow on the podcast. We talk about her Olympic gold medal, her time training for and racing 70.3’s and PTO races, how she took her run from being average at the world level to being the best in the sport, as well as hearing how her swim & bike are so good and what she did to make that happen. We talk about her coaching, who she is coached by and how they approach their training as a whole. Her future plans for Ironman and all the usual triathlon chat.

item.title: Road to the PTO European Open - Episode 8
item.itunes:title: Road to the PTO European Open - Episode 8
item.description:
With just over 3 weeks to go until the PTO European Open things have really ramped up! Mika is back and gives an update on his broken leg and what actually went down, Aaron Royle goes over his recent aero testing, Fred keeps putting week after week of great training together &  the boys discuss the Ibiza after party. All of that (and of course the usual training week breakdowns) in this weeks episode of Road to the PTO European Open!

item.itunes:author: Jack Kelly

item.title: Rudy Von Berg - Training Under The Norwegian Triathlon Training Method
item.itunes:title: Rudy Von Berg - Training Under The Norwegian Triathlon Training Method
item.description:
Rudy Von Berg takes us inside the training camp at Kona he did with Lionel Sanders, Collin Chartier and their coach Mikal Iden. We then talk about the training he did under former coach Luc Van Lierde (a 2 x Ironman World Champion himself) that took Rudy to a podium at the Ironman 70.3 World Champs in 2019 and how it differs from the training he and Mikal Iden have been doing in the lead up to Ironman Texas this year. Rudy then gives us a detailed breakdown of the 2023 Nice Ironman World Champs course and breaks down all his main competitors and why he thinks they can or can’t win the race.

item.title: Road to the PTO European Open - Episode 7
item.itunes:title: Road to the PTO European Open - Episode 7
item.description: Well, this is officially the craziest week of Road to the PTO European Open so far. Collin is injured and it's looking unlikely he will be ready to race and Mika had a broke crash and was in hospital when the episode was being recorded. So as the last man standing Frederic Funk called in Aaron Royle & Tom Bishop to fill in. Both of these two are athletes who will be at the front of the race so it was exciting to hear more from them about how they are preparing and their thoughts on the race.

item.itunes:author: Jack Kelly

item.title: John Rodgers - Legendary Swim Coach of Jan Frodeno
item.itunes:title: John Rodgers - Legendary Swim Coach of Jan Frodeno
item.description:
John Rodgers is one of the most decorated and famous swim coaches the world has ever known. He is the swim coach of countless triathletes such as Jan Frodeno, Braden Currie, Josh Amberger, Brad Kahlefelt, Belinda Granger, Tim O’Donnell as well as being a long time head coach of the Australian Olympic Swim Team. JR, as he’s known, is 85 years old and has been coaching for 54 years. He is known for being a devoted, loving friend and role model to his athletes while also driving the highest of high standards and setting expectations of true high performance.

item.title: Kat Matthews - 2nd at the Ironman World Championships, The First Female Sub 8 Hours & The Comeback Story.
item.itunes:title: Kat Matthews - 2nd at the Ironman World Championships, The First Female Sub 8 Hours & The Comeback Story.
item.description:
Kat Matthews came 2nd at the Ironman World Champs, broke 8 hours at the sub 8 project, was having one of the best years of anyone in triathlon and was training to win Kona. That was her goal. Then one day while out training she got hit by a car in a horrific accident. This is her story.

item.title: Sam Long & His New Coach Dr. Dan Plews
item.itunes:title: Sam Long & His New Coach Dr. Dan Plews
item.description:
Sam Long, one of the worlds best long course triathletes, joins us with his brand new coach in 2023 Dan Plews. There is something special about having both coach and athlete on an episode. It allows us to dive much deeper into both sides of the training. This is no exception. item.title: Road to the PTO European Open - Episode 4
item.itunes:title: Road to the PTO European Open - Episode 4
item.description: A full day of eating detailed by the boys including Mika eating his muesli out of a flower vase and having 9000 calories in a day. Colin's swimming turning into the Norwegians swimming, wind blowing Mika a full 180 degrees on his bike, lots of training talk and lots more on Week 4 of Road to the PTO European Open.

item.title: Cam Brown - The Hardest Trainer in Triathlon History & E.P.O in 2000’s Ironman World Champs Races
item.itunes:title: Cam Brown - The Hardest Trainer in Triathlon History & E.P.O in 2000’s Ironman World Champs Races
item.description:
Legendary long course triathlete Cam Brown joins us to talk about his fabled 45 hour training weeks and reputation as being the hardest trainer in triathlon history. Cam has come runner up at The Ironman World Championships twice and third twice and was known amongst his peers to be doing the highest volume of training in the sports history. As we find out on the podcast, this was certainly true.

item.title: Gwen Jorgensen - the Olympic Triathlon Champion returns!
item.itunes:title: Gwen Jorgensen - the Olympic Triathlon Champion returns!
item.description:
The 2016 Olympic Triathlon Champion and worlds most dominate triathlete in 2013-2017 left triathlon in her prime to pursue the goal of winning the Olympic Marathon Gold Medal. After 6 years pursuing that goal Gwen decided to return to triathlon to see if she can qualify for and race at the 2024 Olympics. We talk about her training when she was the best triathlete in the world, when she moved to running and now when she’s back in the triathlon world + lots of other chat. 

*Sign up to PATREON to support the show (for only $2 AUD/$1 USD per week it is the main reason the show is still going. We bloody appreciate it!) -https://www.patreon.com/howtheytrain/

item.description:
Join pro triathletes Collin Chartier, Frederic Funk & Mika Noodt as they take us through week 2 of 10 in their training block leading up to the first PTO race of the year - the European Open. This is a detailed look at the day to day training pro long course triathletes do in the build up to a big race. They tell us every single bit of training they do each week.

item.title: Holly Lawrence - Ironman 70.3 World Champion
item.itunes:title: Holly Lawrence - Ironman 70.3 World Champion
item.description:
Holly Lawrence is a former Ironman 70.3 World Champ & 17 x Ironman 70.3 winner. We talk training including detailed looks at her training weeks, structure, sessions and approaches. We also do a real deep dive on nutrition, body weight, body image & eating disorders in triathlon. There's some running shoe chat, some triathlon suit optimising chat and lots of nerdy triathlon stuff!

item.itunes:author: Jack Kelly

item.title: Road to the PTO European Open - Episode 1
item.itunes:title: Road to the PTO European Open - Episode 1
item.description:
Join Collin Chartier (winner of the 2022 PTO US Open), Frederic Funk (world #9 long course triathlete & 5th at the 2022 Ironman 70.3 Worlds and Mika Noodt (4th at the 2022 Ironman 70.3 World Champs) as they take you through their weekly training in the build up to the first major long course triathlon of the year - the PTO European Open in Ibiza on May 6th.  The three boys will talk day by day, session by session through each of their respective training weeks, ask each other questions and chat to each other about all the details of their training. This is triathlon training & life of a professional long course triathlete insight like no other podcast series before.  We will release an episode every week, same time, same day all the way up til the race and conclude the series with an episode the week after the race with a Race Recap episode where the boys will go day by day through every day during race week and culminate in breaking down the race.

If you love the extra content, please consider signing up to support the show on Patreon. For $1 USD per week it makes bringing you this series (and hopefully more like it in the future) possible - https://www.patreon.com/howtheytrain/

item.title: Patrick Lange - 2 x Ironman World Champion
item.itunes:title: Patrick Lange - 2 x Ironman World Champion
item.description:
Patrick Lange is a 2 x Ironman World Champion. He was the first person to ever go under 8 hours at The Ironman World Championships when he broke the course record in 2018. He is also the fastest man ever in an Ironman marathon running 2:30:31 last year at Ironman Israel. In this episode we talk about Patrick's training, racing and life.

item.title: Andrew Messick - Ironman CEO: The Episode Everyone is Talking About.
item.itunes:title: Andrew Messick - Ironman CEO: The Episode Everyone is Talking About.
item.description:
It’s time to listen to the podcast that everyone has been talking about with Ironman CEO Andrew Messick. This episode gets heated. There’s hard questions, debate, there’s back & forth and **spoiler alert** it ultimately ends with Andrew walking off the podcast. But at least he got to eat his lunch live on air before walking off

item.itunes:author: Jack Kelly

item.title: Alistair Brownlee - How Does The Greatest of all Time Train?
item.itunes:title: Alistair Brownlee - How Does The Greatest of all Time Train?
item.description:
Alistair Brownlee is a 2 x Olympic Gold Medalist, 4 x World Champion, 4 x European Champion and Commonweath Games Gold Medalist. He is the most dominant Short Course Triathlete the world has ever seen. No one in the sport of triathlon has ever put in the level of performances Alistair Brownlee did at his peak. He’s also moved to long course and been a 2 x Ironman 70.3 World Champion and  multiple Ironman Champion. We talk about what training Alistair was doing leading into both of his Olympic Gold medal winning races, his training philosophies including the importance of aerobic training, high volume & consistency to success. And as always there’s the exact breakdown of what does a typical Monday -> Sunday training week look like for him. We go into some classic Alistair Brownlee stories, talk about the year ahead and how Alistair plans to win the Ironman World Championships.

** Sign up to PATREON to support the show (for only $2 AUD/$1 USD per week it is the only reason the show is still going. We bloody appreciate it!) -https://www.patreon.com/howtheytrain

Use code 'HTT20' to get 20% off your Pillar Performance order https://pillarperformance.shop/

item.title: Collin Chartier - PTO US Open & Ironman Champion
item.itunes:title: Collin Chartier - PTO US Open & Ironman Champion
item.description:
In todays episode we talk about what training Collin did in the lead up to winning the PTO US Open with coach Mikal Iden & training partner Lionel Sanders. Lionel, Collin and Mikal decided to do a period of overreaching training prior to the PTO US Open in a bid to beat the worlds best - and it worked. We go through the training day by day and then discuss their brutal heat training. Then we talk about the following month in the lead up to the Ironman World Champs where, under the watchful eye of Coach Mikal, Collin & Lionel continued on their path of doing HUGE amounts of training out on the big island. Talbot Cox filmed their training out there and posted about it every day on Lionel Sanders YouTube page. Once you listen to this - go and watch that. We then talk about diet, both day to day and race day. Collin is as honest and detailed as any guest we’ve had and I found this chat with him so interesting, raw & insightful. 

** Sign up to PATREON to support the show (for only $2 AUD/$1 USD per week it is the only reason the show is still going. We bloody appreciate it!) -https://www.patreon.com/howtheytrain

Use code 'HTT20' to get 20% off your Pillar Performance order https://pillarperformance.shop/

item.title: Joe Skipper - 5th at the Ironman World Championships.
item.itunes:title: Joe Skipper - 5th at the Ironman World Championships.
item.description:
For the first episode of 2023 we are joined by one of the shows reoccurring guests & best friends, Joe Skipper. Joe came 5th at the Ironman World Championships a couple of months ago so we chat about that in detail. We then talk about the Ironman World Champs moving locations. Then some training and general triathlon chat. This whole episode took place while Joe was at an airport and is chaotic in the best way.

item.title: Emma Snowsill - Olympic & World Triathlon Champion
item.itunes:title: Emma Snowsill - Olympic & World Triathlon Champion
item.description:
Emma Snowsill is one of the greatest female triathletes to have ever lived. She's an Olympic Gold Medalist and 3 x Triathlon World Champion. We talk all about Emma's training. Exactly what her swim, bike and run training looked like in order to win her Olympic Gold Medal. She takes us through specifics of her weekly training, what every single session specifically looked like, where it all was in her schedule and why. This was as comprehensive a training chat as we've had. We also talk all about her racing, coaches, retirement and then go deep on the mental side of triathlon and how Emma believes the thing that separated her from the rest was her mental toughness. She takes us through this in detail. We briefly touch on her relationship with husband Jan Frodeno and find out if she has any influence on his training. Emma is one of the true legends of triathlon and if you don't know her story and love triathlon, this episode is for you.

** Sign up to PATREON  to support the show (for only $2 AUD/$1 USD per week it is the only reason the show is still going. We bloody appreciate it!) -https://www.patreon.com/howtheytrain

item.title: Max Neumann & Steve McKenna - Ironman Training & Racing Chat With Australia’s Two Best Ironman Triathletes.
item.itunes:title: Max Neumann & Steve McKenna - Ironman Training & Racing Chat With Australia’s Two Best Ironman Triathletes.
item.description:
In today’s episode Max Neumann & Steve McKenna join us to break down their race against each other at Ironman Busselton where they came 1st & 2nd. We hear about the race from both of their perspectives, all of the tactics and things from inside the pro race that you don’t hear about anywhere else. We also talk about their training in the lead up to the race, how they prepared to race each other and how they will change things in their training in the future to be better in 2023. We chat Ironman World Champs moving from Kona and what that means for Max who finished 4th their this year and is one of the favourites to win next year and lots of other triathlon chat! 

** Sign up to PATREON  to support the show (for only $2 AUD/$1 USD per week it keeps the show alive) -https://www.patreon.com/howtheytrain

item.itunes:author: Jack Kelly

item.title: Leo Bergere - The New Olympic Distance Triathlon World Champion
item.itunes:title: Leo Bergere - The New Olympic Distance Triathlon World Champion
item.description:
The World Champion Olympic distance triathlete of 2022 Leo Bergere joins us to talk about his training, racing and life. Leo tells us exactly what he does in training Monday -> Sunday. Every single session, every detail, in the lead up to winning the World Champs. You can literally copy Leos training after this podcast. We talk about his change from short, fast VO2 max style training to a more volume based aerobic program and how that took his racing to the next level. Everyone expected Alex Yee or Hayden Wilde to win the World Championships & Grand Final this year, but Leo shocked the world and won both. Does this make him the favourite for the next Olympics?

Sign up to Patreon to support the show (for only $2 per week it keeps the show alive) -https://www.patreon.com/howtheytrain

item.title: Magnus Ditlev - The Favourite to win Kona in 2023?
item.itunes:title: Magnus Ditlev - The Favourite to win Kona in 2023?
item.description:
Magnus is the #3 ranked mens triathlete in the world. We chat about his Ironman World Championships training & race, 3rd place finish at the Ironman 70.3 World Champs and what he did between Kona and it and then the other big results and training in the lead in to his PTO races. Magnus Ditlev is seen by many people as the favourite to win the Ironman World Championships next year and the guy who could defeat the Norwegians and Jan. Magnus also talks to me about secret bike technology leading into the world champs and how he is going to go even further with it.

Sign up to Patreon to support the show (for only $2 per week it keeps the show alive) -https://www.patreon.com/howtheytrain

item.title: Sam Long - The Ironman 70.3 World Champs Penalty Drama + All Things Triathlon & Training
item.itunes:title: Sam Long - The Ironman 70.3 World Champs Penalty Drama + All Things Triathlon & Training
item.description:
Sam Long joined me for his first podcast since the Ironman 70.3 World Champs penalty drama that took over the sport of triathlon last week. Sam said to me before the episode “any question you ask me, I will answer it honestly.” And so I asked all the hard questions that we wanted to hear the answers to and Sam literally left nothing out. This is one of the most real, honest, inside access chats I’ve ever had on the podcast. We go deep on the 70.3 World Champs penalty and then the Sam Laidlow drama, watching him at Kona, their relationship, their rivalry - all of it. We then talk training with the Norwegians, Lionel Sanders, is there doping in triathlon and Sam gives some shocking drug testing insight. We go over his training in the lead up to 70.3 worlds, his swimming, will he race Ironman branded races next year, his insane long runs and what changes the sport of triathlon needs to make. And there’s seriously way more. This episode is insane.

SUPPORT THE SHOW & SIGN UP TO PATREON - https://www.patreon.com/howtheytrain

Use code 'HTT10' to get $10 off your first Pillar Performance order https://pillarperformance.shop/

item.title: Emma Pallant-Browne - 3rd Place at the Ironman 70.3 World Championships
item.itunes:title: Emma Pallant-Browne - 3rd Place at the Ironman 70.3 World Championships
item.description:
3rd place finisher at the Ironman 70.3 World Champs Emma Pallant-Browne joins us to chat all things training in the lead up to the World Champs. We recorded this episode in the lead up to the race and I saved it because I had this gut feeling she would podium in the race and it would be so good to post the day after the race. As fate would have it, that happened!! It was a gamble because I wouldn’t have had an episode if Emma DNF’d. Emma combined a strong swim & bike with her world class run to move through the field, onto the podium and put together one of the best performances of her career. She is also close to the most likeable person in triathlon, so if you listen and don’t love her, I don’t get you! But seriously, Emma finishing on the podium at the World Champs is one of the biggest feel good stories in triathlon this year.

SUPPORT THE SHOW & SIGN UP TO PATREON - https://www.patreon.com/howtheytrain

item.title: Paula Findlay - Training for the Ironman 70.3 World Championships
item.itunes:title: Paula Findlay - Training for the Ironman 70.3 World Championships
item.description:
Paula Findlay, one of the big favourites for this weeks Ironman 70.3 World Championships, joins us to talk all about her training in the lead up. What her exact swim, ride & run weeks look like. How she trains differently to other people, how she’s prepared specifically for the world champs, her big nutrition changes for the race, her competition, race strategy and what it’s like being in a relationship with a fellow triathlete.

Sign up to Patreon - https://www.patreon.com/howtheytrain

item.title: Max Neumann - 4th Place at the Ironman World Championships
item.itunes:title: Max Neumann - 4th Place at the Ironman World Championships
item.description:
Max Meumann shocked the world by coming 4th at the Ironman World Championships and being the only guy who could compete toe to toe with the Norwegians on the bike and run. He gives the best race recap from Kona I’ve ever heard, talks about Kristian Blummenfelt’s aggressive and ruthless racing on the day, his “crazy” training, how he heat prepped for the race, his poor treatment by Ironman Oceania this year and lots, lots more in this 2 hour special edition.

Sign up to Patreon - https://www.patreon.com/howtheytrain

item.title: Taylor Knibb (Ep 63)
item.itunes:title: Taylor Knibb (Ep 63)
item.description:
The Ironman World Championships were yesterday and over the next week we will be recording episodes with the winners and big players. But today, in the meantime, we look ahead to the next big race - the Ironman 70.3 World Champs that take place in 3 weeks time. And Taylor Knibb is the favourite to win that race. She has a world class swim, the best 70.3 bike in the sport and is a solid runner. She’s the athlete you have to get through if you’re any chance. We go through all her training in the build up and find out what training she’s done to become the big favourite for the race.

Sign up to Patreon - https://www.patreon.com/howtheytrain

item.title: Joe Skipper (Ep 62)
item.itunes:title: Joe Skipper (Ep 62)
item.description:
The last day of our five episodes in five days to celebrate the Ironman World Champs with one of my all time favourite people in triathlon, the junkyard dog himself Joe Skipper. So many people are predicting Joe to have a massive race in Kona this weekend. He's in career best form, has the bike/run on his day to beat just about everyone in the world and in this episode he takes us right through the training he's been doing to get in what he hopes is World Championship winning shape. We also have all the usual fun chat that comes with talking to Joe!! Thanks everyone for tuning in this week, I hope you've enjoyed all the episodes and it's made you more excited/ready for the Ironman World Champs hearing about the training blocks the athlete and coaches have done in the lead up. If you haven't listened to them all, then hopefully you've got some long rides, runs or car trips over the weekend to listen to them to help motivate you and make the time fly by!

Sign up to Patreon - https://www.patreon.com/howtheytrain

item.title: Dan Plews (Ep 61)
item.itunes:title: Dan Plews (Ep 61)
item.description:
Dan Plews is one of the worlds best long course triathlon coaches, in this episode we discuss everything that goes into preparing for the Ironman Worlds Champs at Kona. We go deep on every single thing you'd want to know about preparing for this race. The training, the altitude or heat camps, nutrition, what you need to specifically train for in this race vs other races! If you like nerdy, deep dive, detailed training chat then this is the episode for you!

Sign up to Patreon - https://www.patreon.com/howtheytrain

item.title: Skye Moench (Ep 60)
item.itunes:title: Skye Moench (Ep 60)
item.description:
Day three of our Ironman World Champs week is Skye Moench! Skye came 4th at last years World Champs and with the women's race being on Thursday we are only a bit over one day away from seeing if she can better that this year. This chat with Skye is awesome, she takes us deep inside the training she's done in the lead up (and in her career as a whole). Not only do we talk about all of her training in crazy detail, we also chat about diet and weight in potentially the most meaningful way we ever have on an episode. Is looking at yourself and hating what you see normal as a triathlete even if you're in great shape? Are you alone if you have ever had thoughts like that? You're not and hear about it from one of the worlds best professional triathletes in this episode! 

Sign up to Patreon - https://www.patreon.com/howtheytrain

item.title: Daniel Baekkegard (Ep 58)
item.itunes:title: Daniel Baekkegard (Ep 58)
item.description:
Ironman World champs week is finally here!! And to kick off our week of one podcast per day we have Daniel Baekkegard! Daniel was in the lead group of 5 at last years Ironman World Champs for the first 5 hours of the race and came 3rd at last years Ironman 70.3 World Champs. He gave more training details in this episode than any guest ever has in the past. If you're willing to do the 32 hour minimum weeks Daniel does you can literally copy his World Champs prep after listening to this episode! Daniel will be at the front of the World Champs this weekend, that's a given, it's just how long he stays there for that is the question. 

This episode was brought to you by Pillar Performance, use discount code HTT10 for 10% off at checkout https://pillarperformance.shop/

item.itunes:title: Ben Kanute (Ep 56)
item.description:
Ben Kanute might be the most underrated Ironman 70.3 triathlete to have ever lived. He came 2nd at the 2017 70.3 World Championships behind Javier Gomez, 4th in 2018 behind Jan Frodeno, Alistair Brownlee & Javier Gomez and then 6th in 2021 in one of the deepest races in Ironman 70.3 history. He’s also won multiple Ironman 70.3’s in dominant fashion. We talk in extreme detail about the training that got him to that level, the system he and his coach use in their training.

 

Sign up to Patreon - https://www.patreon.com/howtheytrain

item.title: Jess Stenson (Ep 55)
item.itunes:title: Jess Stenson (Ep 55)
item.description:
A detailed look into the training block leading into wining a commonwealth games marathon gold medal. A full race debrief by Jess taking us play by play through her race, the highs, the lows and the inside stories you missed watching it on TV. We also talk race shoes, nutrition and all the little details that go into winning a marathon gold medal.

item.title: Sam Laidlow (Ep 54)
item.itunes:title: Sam Laidlow (Ep 54)
item.description:
Sam Laidlow is a pro triathlete who has made waves in 2022. He raced off the front of the Ironman World Championships and the Canadian Open. He’s also called out other big name triathletes and made it known he is here to be the best. This has led to some drama. We talk about that + his training on todays episode.

 

Sign up to Patreon - https://www.patreon.com/howtheytrain

item.title: Sebastian Kienle (Ep 53)
item.itunes:title: Sebastian Kienle (Ep 53)
item.description:
Sebastian Kienle is one of the greatest triathletes of all time. He is an Ironman World Champion & 2 x Ironman 70.3 World Champion. He was known for being an Uber cyclist and takes us deep inside the insane bike training camps he used to do in the lead up to big races. We talk in depth about all elements of his training, he really does tell us everything he does in training. We also talk the usual good stuff like doping, body weight & nutrition, the mental side of triathlon, etc

 

Sign up to Patreon - https://www.patreon.com/howtheytrain

item.title: Dan Lorang (Ep 52)
item.itunes:title: Dan Lorang (Ep 52)
item.description:
Legendary triathlon coach of Jan Frodeno, Lucy Charles-Barclay, Anne Haug and many other world class athletes Dan Lorang joins us to talk in detail about the training he sets for his athletes. We discuss specifically what each of his major athletes do, how their training is structured, what sessions they do, how much they do. We also talk about his role as Head Coach for Bora Hansgrohe, a Grand Tour winning professional cycling team and go deep on what kind of training a GC contendor does and exactly what it looks like. 

 

Sign up to Patreon - https://www.patreon.com/howtheytrain

item.title: Matt Hauser (Ep 51)
item.itunes:title: Matt Hauser (Ep 51)
item.description:
Matt Hauser is one of the best short course triathletes in the world. He recently won the bronze medal at the Commonwealth Games, has been featuring on podiums at WTS events and looks to be the next guy to challenge Alex Yee, Hayden Wilde and Kristian Blummenfelt as being the king of short course. We talk about how much money triathletes make, how short course training compares to long course, what training Matt did to take the step from being good to being one of the best this season, whether the rumours of the Australian Elite Short Course programs being toxic places to live & train are true and lots more.

 

Sign up to Patreon - https://www.patreon.com/howtheytrain

item.title: Brady Threlfall (Ep 50)
item.itunes:title: Brady Threlfall (Ep 50)
item.description:
Episode 50 is finally here!! Jack talks to fellow Australian podcast host Brady Threlfall from the Inside Running podcast. Brady is a 2:19 marathoner. The two boys chat all things from behind the scenes of starting your own endurance sports podcast, behind the scenes stories, things you might not know, why they do it, their love for the sports the cover and give you some insight into their lives.
